experiences.intentions: There are two boxes and two targets. The box at (2,1) is directly below the target at (1,1), and pushing it up would solve one target immediately. The player is at (2,2), adjacent to the box on its right side, so I am already in perfect position to push left? No, pushing left from (2,2) would move the box from (2,1) into the wall boundary outside the room, impossible. To push the box upward onto the target, I would need to stand at (3,1), but that square is reachable via going down, down, left around the wall opening. A strong first repositioning move is down to start getting behind the left box while avoiding deadlocks. The other box at (3,4) can likely be routed to target (4,2) later. Immediate plan: move down to begin path to (3,1).
